title: Generalize RiskTree into domain-neutral Tray
description: Rename the package/module and specifications from RiskTree to Tray, define Tray abstractly as an array-backed hierarchical aggregation structure, and separate finance-specific behavior from the generic core.
acceptance_criteria: No RiskTree package/module references remain; core specifications and docs are domain-neutral; finance-specific VaR/scenario/exposure behavior is either isolated as an explicitly optional capability or removed by an approved decision; OpenSpec and docs validation pass.
close_reason: Generalized the package, docs, EARS source, OpenSpec capabilities, compiler fixtures, and project vocabulary to domain-neutral Tray; isolated finance under FIN-*; strict spec, docs, test, formatting, typo, and traceability checks pass.

title: Resolve all RiskTree and compiler-IR spec findings
description: Apply the consolidated nine-skill review findings to the RiskTree and compiler-IR OpenSpec changes and authoritative tray-jl-ears-spec.md, including algebra, identity/versioning, transactions, approximation, finite-change semantics, traceability, and lifecycle corrections.
acceptance_criteria: All confirmed findings are addressed; source and deltas agree; both OpenSpec changes pass strict validation; focused specialist convergence review reports no approval blockers.
close_reason: Resolved all confirmed RiskTree and compiler-IR specification findings; synchronized the authoritative EARS source; strict OpenSpec, traceability, typo, and whitespace checks pass. ah reports only expected no-toml implementation-contract gaps for proposed scenarios.

title: Covered IR generation and rejection boundaries
description:
## Execution Mode
AFK — autonomous implementation after all listed blockers are closed.

## OpenSpec Scope
Change: `add-compiler-ir-incrementalization`.
Requirement IDs: REQ-A3, REQ-A8, REQ-A10, REQ-A15.
Exact OpenSpec files: `openspec/changes/add-compiler-ir-incrementalization/proposal.md`, `openspec/changes/add-compiler-ir-incrementalization/design.md`, `openspec/changes/add-compiler-ir-incrementalization/tasks.md`, and `openspec/changes/add-compiler-ir-incrementalization/specs/compiler-ir-incrementalization/spec.md`.

## End-to-End Behavior
Generate incremental IR only inside the covered subset and reject unsupported control/data-flow before emitting an artifact.

## Likely Files / Systems
`src/Tray.jl`; anticipated focused `src/` modules for this capability; `test/` focused and integration suites; `openspec/changes/add-compiler-ir-incrementalization/proposal.md`, `openspec/changes/add-compiler-ir-incrementalization/design.md`, `openspec/changes/add-compiler-ir-incrementalization/tasks.md`, and `openspec/changes/add-compiler-ir-incrementalization/specs/compiler-ir-incrementalization/spec.md`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All covered IR fixtures generate and match reference finite changes; every boundary fixture is rejected before artifact emission with its specified reason.
- The focused tests for REQ-A3, REQ-A8, REQ-A10, REQ-A15 pass and demonstrate the end-to-end behavior at the public API boundary.
- `just test`, `ah check --changes add-compiler-ir-incrementalization`, `openspec validate add-compiler-ir-incrementalization --strict`, and `git diff --check` all exit 0.

## Focused Tests and Verification
Write focused unit, failure-path, and end-to-end tests under `test/` for REQ-A3, REQ-A8, REQ-A10, REQ-A15; then run `just test`, `ah check --changes add-compiler-ir-incrementalization`, `openspec validate add-compiler-ir-incrementalization --strict`, and `git diff --check`.

## Blockers
Blocked by actual dependencies: `TRAYS-ecx` (Finite-change kernel and exact primitive rules), `TRAYS-fle` (Immutable incremental-rule registry), `TRAYS-sif` (Fail-closed provider and analysis API). Do not start until every listed dependency is closed.

CRITICAL: Follow Test Driven Development and Tidy First workflows. Write tests before implementation; clean up related code before adding functionality.
acceptance_criteria: All covered IR fixtures generate and match reference finite changes; every boundary fixture is rejected before artifact emission with its specified reason.

title: Atomic compiler strategy integration with Tray
description:
## Execution Mode
AFK — autonomous implementation after all listed blockers are closed.

## OpenSpec Scope
Change: `add-compiler-ir-incrementalization`.
Requirement IDs: REQ-A7, REQ-A9.
Exact OpenSpec files: `openspec/changes/add-compiler-ir-incrementalization/proposal.md`, `openspec/changes/add-compiler-ir-incrementalization/design.md`, `openspec/changes/add-compiler-ir-incrementalization/tasks.md`, and `openspec/changes/add-compiler-ir-incrementalization/specs/compiler-ir-incrementalization/spec.md`.

## End-to-End Behavior
Integrate callable compiler strategies into Tray updates atomically so derivation/execution failure cannot expose partial aggregate state.

## Likely Files / Systems
`src/Tray.jl`; anticipated focused `src/` modules for this capability; `test/` focused and integration suites; `openspec/changes/add-compiler-ir-incrementalization/proposal.md`, `openspec/changes/add-compiler-ir-incrementalization/design.md`, `openspec/changes/add-compiler-ir-incrementalization/tasks.md`, and `openspec/changes/add-compiler-ir-incrementalization/specs/compiler-ir-incrementalization/spec.md`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Successful generated updates equal full recomputation; injected derivation or execution failures expose no private partial path and atomically publish the complete canonical recomputation for the requested update.
- The focused tests for REQ-A7, REQ-A9 pass and demonstrate the end-to-end behavior at the public API boundary.
- `just test`, `ah check --changes add-compiler-ir-incrementalization`, `openspec validate add-compiler-ir-incrementalization --strict`, and `git diff --check` all exit 0.

title: Approve mapped binary compatibility policy
description:
## Execution Mode
HITL — a human must approve the specified design decision before implementation proceeds.

## OpenSpec Scope
Change: `add-tray-capabilities`.
Requirement IDs: REQ-24, REQ-26.
Exact OpenSpec files: `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/proposal.md`, `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/design.md`, `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/tasks.md`, and `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/specs/consistent-sharing/spec.md`.

## End-to-End Behavior
Produce an approved, implementable policy that fully determines behavior for REQ-24, REQ-26, so downstream implementation has no unresolved semantic choices.

## Exact Decision
Fix format versioning, byte order, primitive types/layout, synchronization protocol, cutover/backward-compatibility policy, and required golden fixtures.

## Alternatives Considered
Compare explicit-version headers and migration/cutover strategies under cross-process atomicity, endian/type stability, crash rollback, old/new reader interoperability, and fixture longevity; implementation-defined native layout is forbidden.

## Rationale
The selected policy must remove implementation ambiguity while preserving every normative guarantee, and approval is required because changing it later would alter persisted or externally observable compatibility semantics.

## Decision Artifact
An approved update to `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/design.md` or a linked approved ADR containing the byte-level schema, state machine, compatibility matrix, and fixture obligations.

## Approval Criterion
A named human approver records approval; every field has byte offset/type/order, every supported version pairing has a compatibility result, and synchronization/cutover/rollback plus golden fixtures are fully specified.

## Likely Files / Systems
Decision concerns `src/Tray.jl`, anticipated focused `src/` modules, `test/` fixture/conformance systems, and `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/proposal.md`, `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/design.md`, `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/tasks.md`, and `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/specs/consistent-sharing/spec.md`; this issue changes only the approved design update/ADR artifact.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A named human approver records approval; every field has byte offset/type/order, every supported version pairing has a compatibility result, and synchronization/cutover/rollback plus golden fixtures are fully specified.
- The artifact maps every choice to REQ-24, REQ-26, states rejected alternatives, and defines binary conformance tests.
- `just test`, `ah check --changes add-tray-capabilities`, `openspec validate add-tray-capabilities --strict`, and `git diff --check` all exit 0 after the artifact update.

## Focused Tests and Verification
Specify the focused conformance/fixture tests downstream implementation must add under `test/`; validate the decision artifact with `just test`, `ah check --changes add-tray-capabilities`, `openspec validate add-tray-capabilities --strict`, and `git diff --check`.

## Blockers
No prerequisites. This human approval gate unblocks `TRAYS-bn5` (Cross-process sharing and rollback-safe persistence).

CRITICAL: Follow Test Driven Development and Tidy First workflows. Write tests before implementation; clean up related code before adding functionality.
acceptance_criteria: A named human approver records approval; every field has byte offset/type/order, every supported version pairing has a compatibility result, and synchronization/cutover/rollback plus golden fixtures are fully specified.

title: Approve compressed-sample algorithm and guarantees
description:
## Execution Mode
HITL — a human must approve the specified design decision before implementation proceeds.

## OpenSpec Scope
Change: `add-tray-capabilities`.
Requirement IDs: REQ-21, REQ-22, REQ-32, REQ-44.
Exact OpenSpec files: `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/proposal.md`, `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/design.md`, `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/tasks.md`, and `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/specs/sample-analytics/spec.md`.

## End-to-End Behavior
Produce an approved, implementable policy that fully determines behavior for REQ-21, REQ-22, REQ-32, REQ-44, so downstream implementation has no unresolved semantic choices.

## Exact Decision
Choose a conforming aligned-sum sketch, its promotion/source-reconstruction procedure, rank-uncertainty guarantee, and bounded-storage formula.

## Alternatives Considered
Compare candidate aligned-sum sketches under exact alignment, deterministic promotion/reconstruction, explicit rank-error bounds, merge/update behavior, and the normative storage bound; no choice may weaken REQ-21/22/32/44.

## Rationale
The selected policy must remove implementation ambiguity while preserving every normative guarantee, and approval is required because changing it later would alter persisted or externally observable compatibility semantics.

## Decision Artifact
An approved update to `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/design.md` or a linked approved ADR, with algorithm, invariants, pseudocode, bounds, and test oracle.

## Approval Criterion
A named human approver records approval of one complete algorithm; the artifact contains no unresolved algorithmic choice and gives a mechanically checkable rank-uncertainty and storage bound.

## Likely Files / Systems
Decision concerns `src/Tray.jl`, anticipated focused `src/` modules, `test/` fixture/conformance systems, and `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/proposal.md`, `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/design.md`, `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/tasks.md`, and `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/specs/sample-analytics/spec.md`; this issue changes only the approved design update/ADR artifact.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A named human approver records approval of one complete algorithm; the artifact contains no unresolved algorithmic choice and gives a mechanically checkable rank-uncertainty and storage bound.
- The artifact maps every choice to REQ-21, REQ-22, REQ-32, REQ-44, states rejected alternatives, and defines binary conformance tests.
- `just test`, `ah check --changes add-tray-capabilities`, `openspec validate add-tray-capabilities --strict`, and `git diff --check` all exit 0 after the artifact update.

## Focused Tests and Verification

## Blockers

title: Multi-skill evaluation of RiskTree specifications

close_reason: Completed nine-skill parallel evaluations for RiskTree and compiler-IR specifications, cross-validated findings, and consolidated readiness results.

title: Cross-process sharing and rollback-safe persistence
description:
## Execution Mode
AFK — autonomous implementation after all listed blockers are closed.

## OpenSpec Scope
Change: `add-tray-capabilities`.
Requirement IDs: REQ-24, REQ-26.

## End-to-End Behavior
Share mapped Tray state across processes and persist commits so crashes/failed cutovers recover the last complete compatible revision.

## Likely Files / Systems
`src/Tray.jl`; anticipated focused `src/` modules for this capability; `test/` focused and integration suites; `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/proposal.md`, `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/design.md`, `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/tasks.md`, and `openspec/changes/add-tray-capabilities/specs/consistent-sharing/spec.md`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Two-process tests observe only complete revisions using approved golden fixtures, and crash injection at every cutover phase recovers exactly the previous or new committed revision.
- The focused tests for REQ-24, REQ-26 pass and demonstrate the end-to-end behavior at the public API boundary.
- `just test`, `ah check --changes add-tray-capabilities`, `openspec validate add-tray-capabilities --strict`, and `git diff --check` all exit 0.
